summary: Excellent soundboard recording of the October 26 1980 Radio City show – crisp audio and a legendary acoustic set with standout Iko, He’s Gone, Brokedown Palace and more.
review: The Hinko‑sourced soundboard captures the night with crystal‑clear clarity; after an initial audience mic hiccup the mix settles into a sweet, balanced sound that many reviewers call “better than the released live album.” Musically the concert is hailed as one of the band’s best – a nine‑song acoustic opener followed by an electric set packed with Iko, Jack Straw, He’s Gone, Brokedown Palace and a fiery Little Red Rooster.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (standout acoustic set, energetic electric songs) and recording quality (soundboard source, crisp/sweet mix), with no significant complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Initial audience mic on the acoustic Iko is noted but quickly switches to clean soundboard feed; overall mix described as crisp, sweet and better than official releases.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A nine‑song acoustic opener sets a mellow, intimate tone before the band launches into a high‑energy electric second set; the transition is seamless and keeps the crowd fully engaged.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as close to its best and his playing “living large” during this period.
band_member_comments.Phil: Sparingly used but strong bass noted in the acoustic set.
